data.title: Senators introduce bipartisan bill to promote employee ownership for retirement security creating stable jobs.
data.description: On July 25, 2025, in Washington, a bipartisan effort was made as Senators Steve Daines from Montana representing the Republican party, and Maggie Hassan from New Hampshire representing the Democratic party, put forth a new bill in the Senate. This legislation aims to streamline the process for establishing additional privately-owned businesses by employees, with a focus on boosting job opportunities, enhancing retirement savings, and fostering growth in the business sector. The proposal seeks to encourage the creation of more private employee-owned companies to provide a secure source of employment, financial stability for retirement, and to bolster the overall business landscape.

data.title: xonPlus launches real-time breach alerting platform in Chennai, India on July 25, 2025, to detect credential exposures and data breaches for immediate response.
data.description: In Chennai, India, on July 25, 2025, a real-time digital risk alerting system called xonPlus has been launched. This system aims to aid security teams in identifying credential exposures before attackers can misuse them. By detecting data breaches promptly, xonPlus notifies teams and systems, enabling instant responses to mitigate risks.

data.title: ACCEL Schools appoints Dr. William J. Bennett as founding provost of new Classical Academy network in McLean, VA on July 25, 2025 - bold step in reimagining virtual education.
data.description: In McLean, Virginia on July 25, 2025, ACCEL Schools appointed Dr. William J. Bennett, ex-U.S. Secretary of Education and renowned author of The Book of Virtues, as the Founding Provost of a fresh network of Classical Academies. This move marks a significant leap in reshaping virtual education by infusing it with classical principles and approaches.

The stock market has continuously been on an upward trajectory, with notable companies making significant gains amidst ongoing trade negotiations and corporate announcements.

data.title: Dow up after surprise data, Tesla boosts stock with robotaxi plans. - Investor's Business Daily
data.description: Following unexpected economic data, the Dow Jones index experienced a growth while Tesla's stock saw a surge, driven by the announcement of its forthcoming robotaxi service. The significant rise in the Dow Jones was noted after a meeting between Trump and Powell, illustrating positive market sentiment. This news reflects the dynamic movement in the market today, highlighting the resilience and adaptability of investors amid changing circumstances.

data.title: Volcon Inc. boosts Bitcoin holdings to 3,183 BTC worth $375 million, signals belief in Bitcoin as long-term asset.
data.description: Volcon Inc. (NASDAQ: VLCN) is intensifying its shift towards Bitcoin by disclosing its ownership of more than 3,183 Bitcoin (CRYPTO: BTC/USD), valued at around $375 million as of July 25. Originally focused on electric off-road vehicles, the company is now adopting a treasury strategy centered on digital assets. Co-CEO Ryan Lane stated that this move demonstrates Volcon's confidence in Bitcoin as a resilient long-term asset and a wise reserve option. By 8 a.m. ET on Thursday, Volcon had purchased 3,183.37 BTC at an average cost of $117,697 per coin. In addition to its robust crypto acquisition, the company's board authorized a substantial expansion of its stock buyback program.

data.title: Federal Home Loan Bank of Des Moines reported Q2 2025 financial results: $194M net income, $21M AHP assessments, $114.8B in advances, and $145M dividend declared.
data.description: The article covers the financial highlights of the Federal Home Loan Bank of Des Moines for the second quarter of 2025. Some key numbers include a net income of $194 million, Affordable Housing Program assessments of $21 million, voluntary contributions of $43 million, advances totaling $114.8 billion, mortgage loans held for portfolio at $13.2 billion, and letters of credit at $17.7 billion. The bank made dividend payments totaling $145 million, and its total assets increased to $190 billion. The Bank aims to provide liquidity for housing and economic development needs, with its housing and community programs playing a vital role. The Bank contributes to the Affordable Housing Program and offers voluntary programs to support affordable housing initiatives. Financially, the Bank recorded a net income of $194 million for the quarter and $399 million for the six months ending June 30, 2025. Total capital increased to $10.2 billion by June 30, 2025.
